description: The 'Helm' (also Great Helm in the file name) is the default Helmet. It is found on the player when they first wake up in Visken's basement.
Askanii Economic Freestrider Accord. The Askanii version of an EFA.
the Azgal Folkhem Forge. The dwarves' old Azgal underground empire.
short for Alignment Intent Studies. It's a very specialized field of study that focuses on the esoteric effect of alignment, it's practical uses, and the various ways to interact with it.
means 'as soon as possible'. Silly modern slang.
Abacus was a gnome of legend of the Gate Era. He, along with the Mist Titan Kha-Ymor, was credited with slaying the dragon Esuth, as told in the book Folk Tales found in Visken's Lair.

Abilities are Esoteric Ebb's primary character statistics — six attributes which represent how good a character is at given tasks, namely by modifying values of a dice roll. This system is based on Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition, although streamlined.
#REDIRECT Abilities#Ability_Check
#REDIRECT Abilities#Ability_Score_&_Modifier
#REDIRECT Abilities#Ability_Score_&_Modifier

the infamous esoteric walls of protective spells kept up by thousands of war wizards and clerics during the early Coast War. They were depreciated due to anti-magic methods during the late stage battles.

one of the many theocratic cities which fell towards the end of the Divine Era. While little information about them survived the Arcane Era, what is remembered in history books is their strict adherence to a set of virtues.
Viira is an orc from Bloodcity Bay in the employ of Askanii-Reeds Trading Company, a Freestrider organization. She is found outside of Visken's Lair, south of Alfoz.
those who live off the earth. Simple farmers.
Akzel Mâddson is a Dwarf archeologist.
a city found deep within western Hae'Xi. Known for its rare breed of predatory felines which spread across Vourgeni, as well as a highly complex governmental system.

one of the last Urthfolk houses to arrive after the founding of Norvik. They originally went east during the Coast War, but a handful of them survived and fled to Norvik with several Arcanist artifacts.
Alfoz B.E. is a dwarf and a leading figure in Tolstad's Azgalist movement.
#REDIRECT Alfoz
#REDIRECT Alfoz
everyone's got one. Some spells can detect it.

a children's game where you get your fingers pricked by a make-shift alligator mouth if you press the wrong tooth. A rare toy these days, as the last factory producing in Bloodcity Bay shut down due to an above-board amount of haunted copies being sold across the Coast.
#REDIRECT Ambassador Ivcx Liuvben
Ambassador Ivcx is a Celestial of the Golden Horde. He can temporarily be found in the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building as part of the Freestrider quest Capital Dominion.
a translucent magical resin with a faint yellow glow. It is often used to store esoteric energy and is an extremely valuable material across the Coast.
The Amethyst is a Trinket given by the Hail Snail after freeing him.
highly suspicious.
An Easy Riddle is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.
an ambush predator insectoid. Usually very large and deadly.
"Antesepalian" means "before the closing of the gates", used to describe civilization during the Gate Era, before Jor closed the portals to The Coast, 2027 years before the events of the game.
a fancy name for frog-folk and toad-folk. You've heard they don't like those terms for some reason.
#REDIRECT Snurre Appledent
one of the three pillars of magic. It is represented by the forces that make up all esoteric events.
The Fourth Era. It lasted for 511 years, ending 27 years prior to the events of the game.
a second level Abjuration spell. It locks entryways until dispelled.
Arcanist is one of six possible feats that can be obtained from the quest Election Week. It is related to the Arcanist political party, and is associated with Intelligence.
Arcanists are a magocratic (i.e. autocracy of mages) political ideology, movement, and party that centers around anti-democratic rule of the most powerful wizards.
leader of the infamous Weckish death squad and founder of the proto-fascist post-Arcanist rule in Savalni. A man synonymous with mass killings, tyranny, and general badness.
Arn is a human child working at the Pillar Kiosk next to the Pillar of Jor in Tolstad. He sells equipable newspapers and magazines.
one of eight bands along the Coast. It is a cold, mountainous realm.
Askanii (sometimes Askan) is one of the eight bands along The Coast. It neighbors Reeds and Divinii Torum.
Askanii-Reeds Trading Company is a Freestrider-aligned business and organization, one of many throughout The Coast with multiple branches. In Tolstad their head-quarters are located in Waterlane.
The Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building is a location in Waterlane. It is owned by the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company.
an old noblewoman who was in all the papers a year back. She had lived with a horde of strange mimics for several years, and everyone just thought she was crazy.
Aura of Vitality is a 3rd-level, Primal spell.
Aventail is a Helmet looted from the body of the Skeleton.
a fire elemental in the vague shape of a dwarf. They once held a kingdom on the Undercoast, until they were broken by the Invasion of Ghostly Ghouls during the late Divine Era.
Azgal Ashmask was a low caste dwarf born in the tunnels of Lekm-Muraz. In 443 AE, he became the God of exiles and egalitarianism after starting a workers' revolution.
the name of the official Undercoast state ruled by Azgal Ashmask and his Azgalists until his untimely fading.
an offshoot of Azgalists pushing more extreme parts of the ideology.
Azgalian Spirit is one of two possible feats obtained from the quest Circle Square Conflict. It is ideologically related to the Azgalian Worker's Party.
Azgalian Worker's Party are a political party in Norvik, part of the Azgalist movement. They are labor-oriented and base their ideology in the ideas of its founder Azgal Ashmask.
Azgalian Worker's Party are a political party in Norvik, part of the Azgalist movement. They are labor-oriented and base their ideology in the ideas of its founder Azgal Ashmask.
the workers who built this city want to rule it. Seems only just.
#REDIRECT Azgalian Worker's Party

an esoteric divide splits the circular Coast of Jor into eight geographic areas, commonly known as 'bands'. Each band has unique esoteric and natural traits, and stretches from the Coast to the Infinite Wastes.
Eight esoterically divided geographical areas that makes up The Coast. Each band is distinct in both geography and inhabitants.
a black feathered medium-sized goose. Known to consume travelers up in Tol's Pass.
The Basinet is a Helmet found in the Secret Tunnel.
the battle that determined the end of the Coast War, on the 14th of Chronus, 481 AE. This was the day the Arcanists fell, truly fell, and have not risen since.
term for those creatures invited by Jor during the Gate Era. Essentially, a term for all the extremely old and ancient creatures across the Coast.
a term for the sentient folk who aligned with Jor during the Gate Era. Also known as the antesepalian folk.
the OLD name for the the GREAT SERPENT.
one of the big dwarf clans. They funnel tons of Penning into Norvik.
bikes are nice.
Big Leader Gourgalash is a Frost Troll that can be found in the Undercoast Path. He is first encountered while asleep, and will not wake up unless the Cleric approaches or attacks him.
a legendary text written in the blood of the Drake Slayer himself. It was given to his thirteen commanders on his deathbed.
Blodcity Bay is the largest city of Fell Tire. From the little information in the game, it seems to be home of Evil-aligned people such as Devils or Orcs.
a solemn holiday, usually spent visiting graves of loved ones, especially fallen soldiers. Each day of a week you're supposed to pray for a different fallen soldier.
the human settlement that is settled closest to the Wastes, up in Tol's Pass.
a city-wide effort in 11 PE to stop necromancers from outcompeting mortal-staffed factories.

term: Book of the Mind, Sixth Edition
description: The Book of the Mind is a piece of Literature. It may be acquired during the quest The Lost 6th Edition.
Borgo Darrow was the first of the noblemen House Darrow and the first paladin of the god Urth. He founded the Urthguard, and was said to be brave and invulnerable in battle.
the Last of the Titans. Fought against dragons in Vourgeni.
a theoretical entity made out of the collected thoughts of the dead, said to be one of few aberrations living inside the phlogiston layer.
The Bracers of Thievery are a Trinket found in Visken's Lair on a stone platform after the Burned Corpse is brought to it.
an Askanii tree known for its impervious nature. Sometimes called Ironwood.
Braxo is an imp legal scribe in Tolstad, and a minor but helpful character in the demo. He sits on a pillar south-west of Visken's Lair.

known as the most incompetent Arcanist to have ever studied the Strings. Ruled House Prax for six days before he managed to anger a local lich by being unable to pronounce his name correctly in a public address.
the currency of Bloodcity Bay and its subjects. They come in both paper form and soul-infused iron coins.
The Broken Sword is a Weapon found in the lower part of Visken's Lair, north-west of the room, on a barrel, after dealing with the Zombie (Visken's Lair).
The Bronze Shield is a Trinket that can be found in front of an abandoned hut in the Undercoast Path.
the largest public scholarly facility in Norvik. Supported by the state, and often the location of political upheaval these days, due to student protests.
Brrr is a sentient mushroom encountered in the Undercoast Path. He is first encountered while frozen in an Esoteric Pocket created by a frost troll.
degenerative slang for 'brute', originating from the deep corners of Sideways City, an urban sprawl located in the far north of Reeds.
hair fibres taken from an Askanii mega-ox. Not as fancy as it sounds.
The Bucket is a Helmet. It can be found in Tolstad South, on a platform above the Imposing Tree.
the landshark predator. They burrow through the air with intense speeds.
bôrgare. The craftsmen, merchants, and notably higher standing citizens, in comparison to those of rural roots.
a vast piece of Coastline just east of Norvik. It's a charred area filled with volcanic activity and kobolds.
Caelum Diminorii was a member of the Diminorii and a legendary figure. He was known as the First Arcanist.

Caelum's Mage Hand is a cantrip that can be obtained from using Comprehend Languages on a spell tome within the upper level of the Visken's Lair. It is a modified version of Mage Hand that has been created by Caelum Diminorii.

Caelum's Mage Hand is a cantrip that can be obtained from using Comprehend Languages on a spell tome within the upper level of the Visken's Lair. It is a modified version of Mage Hand that has been created by Caelum Diminorii.

Cantrip in Esoteric Ebb and Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition is a "0th level" spell available at first level. It does not consume a spell slot, so it can be cast freely, unlimited by spellcasting rules of resting.
The Caretaker is a Weapon held by The Duck. It can be pickpocketed at DC 22, however, doing this will trigger an interaction where Caretaker resists and The Duck takes it back.
the famous halfling rebel, fighting against Celestial occupation on the Golden Line.
Charisma (abbr. CHA) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.
Charm Person is a utility spell. It is given by Visken during dialogue with him, or can be pickpocketed from him.

chaotic celestial creatures, often mistaken for aberration due to their erratic physical manifestations. In the far past, they used to patrol the peaks and valleys of Old Diminor, ruthlessly destroying evil beings they came across.
reflections of your esoteric inside.
a nationalistic term for the Urthfolk. Mostly used by lovers of Urth and young men who want to feel like they're not just exploited fools in a system controlled by the Freestriders.
#REDIRECT Esoteric_Ebb#Chris_Bodegård

any individual who completes a full turn around the Coast is rewarded with an esoteric boon, suspected to be from Jor himself. This usually grants the user the ability to cast the cantrip Prestidigitation at will.
short for Citizen's Registry. Usually carried in the form of a small piece of paper.
a privileged political and legal status afforded to landed Urthfolk, burghers, and members of the aristocracy.
short for 'The City Below the City'. A phrase used to describe the incredible amount of tunnels and ruins below Norvik.
Lord Scholar Fetternax Cividix, a famous Reedsian who pioneered psychoanalysis. He died about twenty years ago.
a term for the unified halfling states in western Askan. The official name is the Fordnip Commonwealth, although many avoid this term as to not annoy envoys of the Golden Horde.
in 13 PE (fourteen years ago) a fragile peace was signed in the Halfling Clanlands to the west of Norvik. The Golden Horde have since been a powerful ally of the city.
a servant of a god, deeply devoted and spiritually connected. Typically a divine spellcaster given Power through their deity.
A nearly century spanning conflict fought between the great nations of the Arcane Era. It was unimaginably destructive, with around a half of humankind perishing during it.
The Magistrate handling minting, trade, financing of public works, and tithes. Currently held by Nicholas Darrow in the Tolstad District.
Coinlord's Meet is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
Coinlord's Meet is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
aliases: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

description: Cold Corpse is an interactable corpse in the lower part of Visken's Lair. It starts the quest Three Hundred Pieces.
The Collected Chants of Norvik is a work of Literature located in on a bookshelf in Visken's Lair. It can read while on the bookshelf.
Comprehend Languages is a utility spell. It can be purchased from the Storm Gnome vendor for 100 Crowns.

Concentrated Rhubarb Ale is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Charisma by +1 for a week. 2 bottle of Concentrated Rhubarb Ale can be found around Tolstad: one can be pickpocketed at DC 15 from Rixbiter, and one can be bought from the Potion Seller for 230 Crowns.
slang for conscientious objector. During the Coast War they had a whole penal troop called the Conchie Corp, full of people who were Charmed into performing war-services.
a small political party in Norvik. They stand for traditional values, outside the typical theocratic Nationalist rhetoric.
Constitution (abbr. CON) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.
an area designated as confinement for bodies to avoid the risk of undeath. Many were erected beneath Norvik during the years of attempted reclamation of the Curselands.
Vourgeni slang for 'I understand you, my friend and/or client.'
slang for law enforcement in Reeds. Originates from the copper plates that early Freestrider guardsmen wore around their necks.

a newspaper printed on magical copper sheets. The quality of the final newspaper suffers when using this method of production, but its popularity has skyrocket with indie papers around the Coast using it.
Copper Press Fever is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.
winged snake-like celestials. Messengers and servants of an antesepalian deity and/or titan, sometimes called the World Serpent.
a 3rd level Abjuration spell. A quick, reactive spell capable of fizzling any other spell the caster notices.
the coolest mythical warrior of Old Diminor. Went extinct with the failure of the Cowboy Rebellion.

a minor footnote in the early Coast War, when a union of cow herders in Old Diminor revolted against one of the first Magocrats. Famously, they were known to make use of intelligent horses for their guerrilla tactics, including using entire battalions of horses equipped with portable rifles.
the goblin traitor. The one who signed the Goblin Treaty, giving away everything to Urth, after the goblin genocide.
one of the thirteen original goblin tribes of Askanii. When moved to Norvik, they were renamed several times, eventually settling on the Halfway Bridge Tribe, or HWBT.
a city in eastern Reeds. One of the few placed untouched by the later stages of the Coast War, and thus a stronghold of early Freestriderism.
rare sentient folk, mainly encountered in the swamplands of Hae'Xi. Extremely deadly.
Croland is mentioned by Visken as a bookbinder residing in Waterlane View of Tolstad, related to the quest Phoenix Folio. A poster can be find in South Tolstad:
Urth Crowns. The official currency minted in Norvik's Riksbank.
Crusader's Mantle is an Evocation spell. It can be found in the City Below, buried under some rocks that can be noticed by Detect Secret.
Crusader's Mantle is an Evocation spell. It can be found in the City Below, buried under some rocks that can be noticed by Detect Secret.
slang for Gelatinous Cube.
Cult Hit is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
Cure Wounds is a basic healing spell. It is the earliest and easiest spell to acquire, found sticking out of the pile of apples in Visken's Lair where The Cleric first wakes up.
a horrid, mutated, and ruined creature. These are a form of troll born from the Curselands, most likely, and have spread to infest various places across Reeds and Askan.
dwarven slang for mother-in-law.

fifty thousand square miles, cursed to forever stand as an esoteric gateway to a pure negative energy plane. For scale, that's about the size of the entirety of the land today held by the Norvikian city-state.
Death in Perpetuity.
Dillan's Last Stand. A classic dive bop that got real popular around the time you were born.
one-handed piercing weapon. Good at making holes in clerics.
dwarven for day-fine. Some might call it legal extortion, but it really depends on the district.
Dancing Light is a cantrip that can be obtained from
the thousands of islets and/or speleothems found across the Undercoast.
derogatory term for the Underdwarves.
one of the original Urthfolk houses to found Norvik with Urth. Their bloodline can still be found in high positions all around the city.
the largest public library in Norvik. You spent many years in your youth inside those stony walls.
a term for warriors of necromancy. Many were created and fought during the Coast War, but most legendary were the Death Knights of the Shadow Lord, during the Divine Era.

The Deck of Many Things is a canonically Legendary magic item in both Esoteric Ebb and Dungeons & Dragons 5th edition media. You can acquire the Deck of Many Things by solving the Gynosphinx's second riddle as part of the quest An Easy Riddle.
said to originate from early Arcane Era civilizations in Old Diminor, it is the rule of majority. Those with suffrage may vote to enable their preferred government.
Democratic Herald is a feat obtained after completing the quest Democratization.
the even softer form of socialism, with a state chosen by the people which controls, or at least regulates, the means of production for the needs of its people.
chaotic evil fiend. They are mostly bloodthirsty monsters and rage-crazed barbarians fallen out of Hae'Xi.

a 1st-level divination spell, which allows the caster to see an esoteric effect around creatures who are not true neutral on the alignment axis. Clerics often use this as a utility, even if alignments are not admissible in Norvikian courts since the Urthfolk Rights Order of 499 AE.
a classic 1st level divination spell. It allows the caster to view magical effect, enchantments, illusions, and other esoterical things clearly within a certain range.
a first level divination spell. It allows the user to notice dangerous substances, both in and outside the body.
a 1st level divination spell, and a modification of the Identify spell, which focuses on detecting impurities and errors in magical potions.
a rare 1st level divination spell. It allows the user to notice illusions, traps, and other hidden and generally secret things within a certain range.
Detect Secrets is a utility spell. It can be extracted from the Pickaxe of Secrets with a DC 14 Intelligence check.
lawful evil fiend. They are lawyers, soul-traders, and either the most efficient type of leaders or the worst type of middle managers.
Dexterity (abbr. DEX) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.
#REDIRECT Abilities#Ability Check

a poem about an adventurer who accidentally kills his companion, and then pays all his treasure to resurrect him. These days it's considered slightly racist, however the music track by the same name is still played around the Coast.
a fourth level conjuration spell. It allows the caster to teleport themself and one willing target up to five hundred feet in any direction.
anyone who uses this term for anything modern is either an anti-Freestrider or lives under a rock.
the common language of man. Spread across the Coast during the Arcane Era to become the lingua dominans in trade and politics.
The Diminorii are one of the most powerful magocratic families in Old Diminor. They were the descendants of Doug Doulin Dim, the Drake Slayer King.
an esoterically infused stone disc which has been engraved with a Spoken Word spell. These have been mass-produced since the mid Arcane-Era.
a 4th level divination spell. It allows the user to exchange one hundred coins to answer one single question about the outcome of a certain future event or activity.
one of the three pillars of magic. It is represented by the sacred, the faith of all folk, their souls, and is granted by gods who once walked among us.
The Third Era. It lasted for 379 years.
Divine Hex is a Spell. It is obtained by forming a Warlock Pact with Ever-Glad.
one of eight bands along the Coast. It is an askew plain, a slope growing into the Sacred Wasteland.
The Docile Stress Cube is a Trinket obtained after defeating the Gelatinous Cube.
the act of not getting hit. You should try it.
The Doom is a sub-area of the City Below the City. It can be accessed through a hidden door in the Crypt.
a technique often used by magical thieves. It involves casting Dimension Door, a spell that teleport yourself and another target in any direction, quite far.
old timey derogatory term for tall folk. A genuinely awfully ineffective insult.
Doug Doulin Dim, the Drake Slayer King, was the first human emperor on the Coast. He got his title from his tendency to slay dragons and liberate their human slaves.
a dragon who goes through the act of achieving lichdom. They are known to be the most terrifying creatures long the Coast, for at least two reasons.
the great conflict which raged between dragons during the Divine Era. It directly caused the power vacuum which was filled by first gods and later arcanists.
a series of horrific genocides during the dragon reigns in Old Diminor. Many dragons demanded religious offerings to their cults, and when not appeased, entire peoples would be destroyed.
an old derogatory term for humans.
Dragon of Tolstad is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
aliases: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

description: primal spellcaster. Often balance keeping actors across and below the Coast.

Druidcraft is a cantrip that can be obtained from Nessan after passing a DC 20 Wisdom check. The DC is lowered by 5 when Nessan "Finds you reasonable" (based on the answer to her question of sustainability) and by 5 when "She pities you and your hungry stomach" (based on an answer where The Cleri...
like agriculturalism, but more liberal.
THIS IS NO DUCK.
one of the original Urthfolk houses to found Norvik with Urth. A big house, with many members across the city.
one of five districts in Norvik. Located in and around the Great Steam Pit, in northwestern Norvik.
stout, short, hairy, loves to dig and build, you know the drill. Heh.
the draining.
the name of a famous death knight in service of the Shadow Lord. Also a term used for people who dress up in dark armor and brood a lot.
an old term for magical amber ore. It is only mined in three locations across the Coast.
scary primal overlords of the forest. Rare in Norvik.
Elven Connection is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.
Elves is one of the races populating Jor's Realm. There are several sorts of elves, like Illym Elves or Pimeä Elves, but they are all characterised by their pointed ears.
not quite Illymian elvish. This is of an older tongue, most likely an elven tribe of Divinii Torum or Vourgeni.
the currency of certain former dragon-colonies in Old Diminor. Made entirely in gold, which makes them quite valuable to this day.
The Emerald is a Trinket pickpocketed from Haelon Bondavol at DC 15. Alternatively, you can convince him to give it to you by mentioning Olzis wants it in order to make a gate key.
a legendary elven pirate queen. She holds several of the most important trading routes across Jor's Sea, and is known for braving even the most esoteric shortcuts with her giant lion deckhands.
divine titans, known as the children of the gods. Some of them survive to this day, often in decayed states.
Encounters are turn-based conflicts (not necessarily physical combat as in Dungeons & Dragons).
an esoterically infused undead creature, more akin to a ghost than a regular zombie. They are mostly harmless, apart from slowly draining folk into exhaustion.
Urthguard members trained in horsemanship. Sometimes referred to as the Knights of Norvik.

modern scholars divide history on JOR into five eras. They use canonically important events as starting points for these eras, although this was easier when the Arcanists of Old Diminor still had a common consensus.
a vast family of dwarves. One of the first Exiled families to make contact with Urth's forces.
the magic of Jor and his strings. The Coast is infused with it.
whenever the weird unstable magic around Jor's Coast makes things a bit too wacky, people tend to say this.

Esoteric Ebb is a narrative cRPG by Christoffer Bodegård (studio Sudden Snail) and collaborators including studios Gibbet Games and Speldosa Interactive, published by Raw Fury. Its game system is based on Dungeons & Dragons and heavily inspired by Disco Elysium and Planescape TormentC.
The name of a phenomenon causing high-level magic to slowly be drained from Jor's Realm. It can be learned about by speaking to Haeron Bondalov and passing a passive Intelligence check.
, a collaborative wiki resource that is open for anyone to edit!}}
Welcome to Esoteric Ebb community wiki!

is a collaborative wiki resource that is open for anyone to edit. You don't need special permission beyond registering an account to edit most pages, and your contributions can grow the wiki and help other players.
! We are currently maintaining  articles.
play the strings.
The Esoteric Lockpick is a Trinket located in the South Caverns. It is looted from the body of a headless rogue.
term for naturally occuring magic caused by the shape and form of Jor's Realm. Sometimes called Wild Magic, or Jor's Discord.
area of weird magic. Seldom found along the Coast.
The Esoteric Pocket Watch is a Trinket found on Visken's desk in Visken's Lair. It is a pocket watch that is perpetually incorrect.
Pockets of space where various different Esoteric effects apply. They are rarely found around the Coast, but become increasingly prevalent the closer one gets to the Infinite Wastes.
great waves of moving Esoteric Pockets. Common close to the Endless Wastes and Jor's Point.
Esuth was an ancient dragon of historical importance. He was discussed most prominently in the book Folk Tales found in Visken's Lair.
you do not know what this is.
Ettir is a city-sworn angel of Tolstad, in service of Magister Darrow. She can be found in front of his house, highest part of Tolstad North.
Ever-Glad is a Trifler. He can be found in the Old Prison far below Tolstad.
The Second Era. It was also known as the Fall Era.
then final moments of the Battle of Thazen, when the floating city descended upon the Coast.
slang for negative consequences.
a dwarven word meaning something along the lines of 'devilry'. Common curseword.
the islands found deep in Jor's Sea, even further than Middle Edge. Known for their aberration inhabitants, and the legendary pirate treasures hidden all around.
the ideology of nationalism, born during the fall of the Magocracies in Old Diminor. The term originates from the Gnomish 'fascio', which means something akin to 'a collection of warriors'.
a follower of Fascism.
in remembrance of the tribulations of the Urhtfolk, back in Old Diminor, a feast is held. Most devout would abstain from sustenance for thirty-six hours in preparation.
1st level transmutation. Puts a cap on the target's falling speed.
an 8th level Enchantment spell. It deals a destructive psychic blow to the target's mind, leaving them in a demented state.
a land of elven ruins with magically fertile lowlands in between. In its center sits Bloodcity Bay, the largest city on the Coast, built out of the corpse (or rather just the head) of Gorgorian.
geomancy. A type of esoteric mysticism from Divinii Torum.
a street found in the Low Valley area of Housing. It has about half a dozen dance halls, and it's pretty much the number one party street in Norvik - at least if you're poor.
Fillius Prax was a leading figure of Arcanist ideology, a mage and an author of Autobiography Prax. His descendant Rubrick Prax is the head of The Arcane Circle.
a 7th level necromancy spell. It causes a single target to be overwhelmed by negative energies.

Signed into action a few years ago, the Fire Prevention Declaration is a governmental attempt to curb the spreading of fires by employing always-ready folk in every district. The response times have been pushed down to less than ten minutes.
a third level evocation spell. Causes fire to emerge at a given point, spreading within a twenty foot radius, filling up available space.
The Flower Crown is a Trinket obtained from Miss Nearly after completing the quest Essence of Inflorescence, or pickpocketed from her at DC 12.
a third level transmutation spell. Causes the target to move themselves freely in all directions, ignoring any gravitational laws.
a great celebration during the short Askanii summer. Many animals are slaughtered, and many treats are baked for this annual celebration.

rights inherent to all sentient beings, regardless of folktype, sex, nationality, ethnicity, language, religion, or any other status. Originally spread by Azgal, and is today a hotly debated topic across the Coast.
concerned with or seeking to promote the welfare of all folk around the Coast.
a dwarven term, meaning 'genocide'. It was originally coined by Azgal himself, who used it to support the idea of folk-rights.

the Magistrate handling agriculture, fishing, city stockpiles, rural communities, poor laws, food safety standards, and organizing festivals. Currently held by Yrkavol Trollmord in the Dvârgby district.
The Fordnip Commonwealth is one of the nations on the Askan, home of the halflings. Category:Locations
one of the leading members of the Norvik Azgal Council.
The Forge of the Furies is an Esoteric Pocket containing a sliver of the elemental plane of fire.
the titled of the court and absolute monarchy of the High Dwarves.
a perfectly square miles-wide urban expanse placed at the center of the Verdure Hills. Nothing grows around it, and there are no entrances.
an old deity still worshipped in some parts of Reeds. She was a goddess of luck and wealth.
The Fourth Apprentice of Jor, or the Nameless Apprentice, was one of Jor's original apprentices.
A name that's mentioned as a witness for the case of The Tea Shop that Blew Up, injured during the explosion. Learning the name starts the quest The Star Witness.
aliases: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

description: a collection of city states in Reeds, all united under the golden banner of Freestriderism. Norvik will join them, one way or another.
a term for the liberated post-war Freestrider-led rebuilt Thazen, arguably the greatest Free City in Reeds.
the new way! People in the east have overthrown the old wizards and gods.
#REDIRECT Revolutionary Freestriders
Freydis is a character with a short role in the demo. She can be found in The Holes.
some powerful creatures have auras that cause fear to those near. To fail means succumbing to mindless terror, along with other annoying effects.
The Frog-Mouth is a Helmet found atop a dresser in Darrow's Nest.
the great line drawn by nomad tribes in Vourgeni. No outsiders are allowed past this line, yet many attempt to push it back.
a tribe of trolls who rose to prominence during the late Arcane Era. Some of the most infamous trolls during the Conquest were of this type: Gourgalash for one, as well as the hold-out, Orgroh.
famous textile industrialist and magical carpet extraordinaire. Got really famous during the Arcane Era.
the Blue Thunder. One of the few dragons who worked with the giants of Vourgeni, and built a cult among its hills.
Future Patriarch is one of two possible feats obtained from the quest Young & Dumb.
a dwarven term for tea and cookies, or something else sweet. Usually used to take a break during work, to the dismay of employers everywhere.
COMPLETE and UTTER despair - of flesh and bone.
the main port in Dvârgby. (GSP stands for Great Steam Pit!) Cool place.
Garden Apple is a healing consumable. It is the basic healing item, healing only 1 HP.
the first Bhan-Emvers to settle in Norvik. Head of the great Norvikian construction project.
The first of the five eras, when Jor crafted Jor's Realm, and the gods fought to bring order. Succeeded by the Fade Era.

the legendary conflict between the Beasts of Jor, the original gods, and the many others who tried to take over JOR towards the end of the Gate Era. We have little information about this conflict, but it is known it led to the closing of the Gates, and began the Fade Era.
The Gauntlets of Ogre Power are a Trinket found in the Secret Tunnel.
an old term for happy.
Geesus was a titan who lived in Askan during the Fade Era. He resembled a giant goose.
a common slang, shortened from 'Geesus'. Often used to exclaim a sense of shock and or annoyance.
a mythical realm of evil. Sometimes called the most inhospitable plane of all existence.
a mindless yet dangerous cubic ooze. They are near impossible to spot due to their translucent nature, and digest anything within their grasp with a horrendously acidic substance.
2nd level necromancy spell. Keeps a corpse from decaying.
divination-based mysticism, based on the molding and shaping of the earth. Mostly used in reference to Divinii Torum religions these days, rather than any type of elemental evocation.
a native Askanii bird. They are an opportunistic feeder upon the Coast, hunting the various large and monstrous fish and crustaceans, as well as scraps left by folk.
a great mock battle is held on the fields north of Norvik, where both gladiatorial duels lead to glory, and fun mud wrestling gives the folk a fun activity to attend.
the nomadic central land of the Loxorii. A white mountain with a bowl-shaped miles-wide valley at its peak.
you always memorize important terms in this way. It comes naturally.
short and green. Got claws and fangs.
a dwarven-built citadel, sitting at the southernmost tip of Tolstad. It is now a goblin sanctuary and greenhouse.
the destruction of the goblin clans of Askanii, by Urth, around fifty years ago. Three clans were then 'integrated' into Norvik, with their new generation being put through human schooling.
Goblins are a short humanoid species found in large number in Tolstad's southern quarter and underground.
Goblon is an elusive graffiti artist famous in Norvik.
God-Emperor Wizard is one of six possible feats obtained from the quest Class Conflict. It comes from repeating that you are a Wizard, and is associated with Intelligence.
Walking gods (or demi-gods) of The Coast are ascended mortals who carried divinity of the original gods residing outside of The Coast.
a phrase from Reeds, meaning 'Gold Coin and Copper Press'. The two main methods of Freestrider success!
remnants of a Celestial Empire in Divinii Torum. Led by a horrifying Choir, a hyper-intelligent light-being, they spread fear throughout their lands, as well as parts of western Askanii.
Golden Line is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
a popular Vourgenian sport. You've never played.
a classic Diminorian fairytale about a boy who was so good he exploded. Literally.
Gorgorian was a Archdevil of the Gate Era.
Gorm Skaldeman is the last appointed paladin of Urth. He became the face of the Party Of Norvik, but went missing months prior to the events of the game.
a village of devils and half-dwarven tieflings up in Tol's Pass. Run by a Black Abishai, Thimmassia Gondora.
sentient and enormous creatures who live along the Kargameshian Coastline. They are treated with great respect, and tales of sailors who disrespected the shell are commonly told.

the powerful esoteric effect keeping a perfect gravity balance between the Coast and the Undercoast. Also used as the name for the specific layer in which the gravity flip occurs, which is around three to five miles beneath sea level on both sides.
an event that took place during the mid-Arcane Era, and lasted approximately fifty years. Arcanists, in need of more manpower, organized mass immigrations from all corners of the Coast.
the day the Urthfolk arrived on the Askanii shores for the first time. Often celebrated by cleaning and preparing boats for the first breaking of the ice.
The Great Perch is a drake-like animal that, due to its association with Urth, is used as a symbol of Norvik. Category:Animals
a period of twelve years, where a mass of dwarves immigrated to the newly reconquered and founded 'Norvik' to be employed in the construction of the city.
a collection of primal wizards in Old Diminor, who left behind their old title of Circle of the Earth. This betrayal is still an open wound for many a druid.
a deep crevice in northwestern Norvik that's connected with the river via a canal. A centerpiece of Dvârgby.
the mythical first creature Jor invited to join him on his newly created realm.
a fifth level Abjuration spell. It undoes one debilitating effect from a target.
a monstrosity born out of the Coast War fallout. Produce acid from their antlers, mimic spoken language, often walk on their hind-legs, and are generally extremely deadly.

standing only slightly taller than the average human, these trolls live among the moss and stone of Askanii woods. They are hunter-gatherers, and seldom organize into anything resembling civilization.

a derogatory term for various monstrous humanoids across the Coast. Mostly referring to orcs, goblins, and trolls, despite the fact that the majority of these creatures do not, in fact, have green skin.
a famous magical beast of Old Diminor. Terribly useful mounts, used by those with the funds to support their breeding and taming.
a feathered, yet land-bound monstrosity native to Askanii. Lives upon the Coast and is famous for its many attacks upon travelers of Norvikian roads.
also known as pyrodust, is an ancient chemical compound famously put to use in the early days of the Coast War when an arcanist found a loophole around the Powdercurse.
a monstrous being often found undertaking missions for the gods. A feminine force capable of seeing through time and space.
the Halfway Bridge Tribe. Commonly called 'The Halfways'.
the united magocracy of the Hagginshags and Diminorii. This was arguably the most powerful of the Arcanist-states, said to rival the strength of warfare during the Gate Era conflicts.
Hae'Xi is one of the eight bands that makes up The Coast. Its name means "Growing Maw" or "Into the Air" in abyssal.
Hae'Xi is one of the eight bands that makes up The Coast. Its name means "Growing Maw" or "Into the Air" in abyssal.
a type of exotic dance routine. You've read about it in magazine tales- it's supposed to be rather erotic.
#REDIRECT Haelon Bondavol
Haelon Bondavol is a demi-lich residing in the Doom below Tolstad. Through a successful DC 24 Intelligence check, the spell Speak with Dead can be learned from him.
a spellcaster focused on communing with powerful primal and esoteric forces. Always feminine.
one of the most powerful magocratic families in Old Diminor. Known for their potions and necromancy.
a small icy mollusk, spread in esoteric weather events. Often wreaks havoc on crops and roofs alike.
short for HWBT, the Halfway Bridge Tribe.
Hammer and Zeal is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
an obscure novella about a Reedsian revolutionary falling for her servant, Steve.
a 1st level transmutation spell. Enforces the Powdercurse on a visible target.
an anarchists movement spawned towards the end of the Coast War. They dealt with abyssal forces, and somehow published 'The Hedonists', an instructional booklet that made its way across the Coast.
The 'Helm' (also Great Helm in the file name) is the default Helmet. It is found on the player when they first wake up in Visken's basement.
Helmets are a type of equipable Item in Esoteric Ebb.
Hex Weekly is a piece of Literature than can be purchased from Arn for 20 Crowns. It can also be pickpocketed from him at DC 10.
a brand of potion, as well as the name of the largest Mega-Coven in Reeds. Led by Joanna, the most powerful Hag on the Coast.

Hierophant Ohpidana is a powerful archdruid in the Undercoast. Ohpidana's chief concern is of "keeping balance" in the Undersea by stopping any powers from developing a hold of the region, both dominant powers of The Coast and civilizations in the Undercoast.
few hold this title in Norvik. It is given to great warriors of Urth who serve the Palace closely.
a series of Trade Companies that help spread the Freestrider ideals across the Coast. There are about a dozen of them currently, each run by a Trade Prince.

a form of thought developed by Azgalian dwarves before their exile. It puts the shape of history upon the clash between social classes, ignoring the great beasts and arcanists who single-handedly shaped this world like soft clay.

Hit Points (abbreviated to HP) is a representation of a character's health; i.e. points that decrease if taking damage (often also from social effects such as embarrassment), and which will trigger a Death Saving Throw when they run out.

a city founded within the confines of a massive Gate Era castle, leaning out over the Coast. Known for the Holswyth Ting, and is essentially just a city built out of a series of complex embassies and gnomish industries.
a convention set up on the first year of every decade to facilitate trade and international cooperation and agreements. It is held in the Mega-Castle of Holswyth, on the Vourgeni Coast.
a short name for the singular infinite mountain set along the coast in Divinii Torum. One of those natural world wonders, or whatever.
The Holy Symbol of Whitemane is a Trinket that is located in the Survey. It is hidden in the mouth of the large cat statue, and can be located by casting Detect Secrets.
slang adopted from cat-speech. It means 'man'.
the Magistrate handling construction, public works, housing, mapping, as well as sewers and waterworks. Currently held by Tondedral Sôderfelt in the Housing District.
House Hagginshags was a powerful magocratic family in Old Diminor. They were the descendants of the Shadow Lord.
House Prax is a magocractic family. The last living member of the family is Rubrick Prax, the head of The Arcane Circle, an Arcanist organization.
the aristocracy ruling over several cities in Hae'Xi. They are famous for being elven, human, or even celestials who intermingled with the demonic forces of the region.
a nickname for The Housing District. Where the poors live.
tall and beautiful, like yourself. Known as the 'Pest of the Coast' by those who are less inclined to reproduce like rabbits.
a metal commonly used by dwarven engineers in the construction of Norvik. It is light, but extremely durable.
the eastern point of the Palace district. The third largest port in Norvik.
elvish for 'human'.
the largest trade port in Kargamesh. Known for its great naval culture, and symbiosis with the local Grand Tortoises, which are utilized as ships.
Illym The Mender is an Elven goddess who established the Illym Empire. The Illym Forest is also named after her.
the great woodland covering most of the border between Askan and Reeds. It is the domain of the Illym elves.
slang for the many types of hallucinatory and performance enhancing herbal remedies brought into Norvik by elven smugglers.
The Incense Bowl is a Trinket found in the City Below the City.

a period of rapid technological, esoteric, and social change that began in Old Dominor between 270 and 340 AE. It marked the shift from agrarian, magic-based, and handcraft economies to industrialized and mechanized production.
Inflict Wounds is a basic damaging spell. It is given by The Duck during dialogue, or can be pickpocketed from him at DC 10.
a term for travel towards the Endless Waste. Away from the Coast.
Intelligence (abbr. INT) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.
pretty self-explanatory historical event. Undead army raised by some unnamed lich.

a term coined by Êirik Smêd, a prominent Arcane Era scholar from Vourgeni. It basically refers to the forces of self-interest and competition that, when operating within a free market, unintentionally guide individual actions toward outcomes that benefit society as a whole.
one of Jor's original apprentices. Little is known of her, apart from her symbol (a brachen branch) being littered among certain Gate Era ruins.
The Ioun Stone, Insight is a Trinket given by a Brrr after saving him in the Undercoast Path.
the name of a legendary artifact crafted by Gate Era artificers. They are doors leading to nowhere, instead capturing any creature in a pocket dimension.
a powerful magic item that can trap and store similarly powerful creatures.
Ironbrush is a character mentioned by Snell as the person who drew the green dragon graffiti on a wall of Visken's Lair, Tolstad South.
common nickname of Brachen wood.
one of the three goblin tribes in Norvik. Commonly called the Water Goblins, due to their river expertise.
The Jacinth is a Trinket obtained after solving the 3 riddles of a Minotaur statue in the City Below the City.
nickname for the Jam Factory on the North Wall. It smells quite lovely when you walk past it.
the mysterious human people of Ym, who live in an isolated kingdom upon great plateaus, deep inland.
one of the original Urthfolk houses to found Norvik with Urth. A small house, but one of the richest.
the lady of the brew. CEO of the Hexbrew Inkorporation, and arguably the most powerful entrepreneur on the Coast.
one of those pirate legends. Many adventurers seek his ship and 'tomb' in the Reedsian wastes.
Jor was a powerful wizard who created the dimension of The Coast, which is why it's also called Jor's Realm or simply JOR.
#REDIRECT The Coast
the term used to describe why spells above ninth level are considered impossible to cast by mortal hands. At least, before the Arcanists broke that limit.

the legendary first gate. Based on archeological evidence, the Beasts of Jor believes Jor to have first entered this realm at this location, at the farthest point of Jor's Sea, right where all the bands collide.
a name for the great sea, which the bands surround.
the leylines of esoteric power that holds the realm together.
The Journal is one of the tabs found to the left of the screen where, when clicked, the map of the present area is displayed and dicovered Glossary Entries are found. Category: Mechanics

a pseudo-nomadic clan of orcs who specialize in a certain craft. Each traces its roots into the service of the Queen-Mother Laghbetta, who gifted their ancestry with a specific magical understanding of said craft.
the currency of the Illym Empire. Looks like a piece of ice.
one of eight bands along the Coast. A land of vast xeric shrublands and deserts.
Kattegatt is a Weapon found on the wall of the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building in Waterlane. It can be stolen with a DC 19 Dexterity check.
Kha-Ymor was a Mist Titan with historical importance during the Gate Era. He was discussed most prominently in the book Folk Tales found in Visken's Lair.
a second level transmutation. Unlocks things.
a collection of celestial poems translated into dwarven. You remember reading this a long time ago.
Kraaid is a crocoliskfolk who lives in The Sewers. He is a cook and hunter, and sells Slogdogs, sausages made of a meat with unknown origin.
dwarven word for low beasts, or critter. Often used as a demeaning term for humans.
a street acronym. Means 'Lack of Ladies'.
magic paintings. Look super realistic.
Lady Chamăr is a human who came to Tolstad from the Holy Mountain. She doesn't play any major role in the demo, and her purpose is enigmatic.
Lady Fair was a god, and an ascended halfling.
Lady Fair is one of two possible feats obtained from the quest Young & Dumb.
a young halfing leads her clan through a mountain pass and defends it for three days and three nights. She is known to be responsible for the founding of the Fordnip Commonwealth.
#REDIRECT Lady Fair (Feat)
#REDIRECT Miska Sageleaf
a halfling-led Torna fishing community a few miles north of Urthport.
Queen Mother of the Blood Orcs. Fought against Gorgorian during his reign, and was victorious.
the second largest settlement in Fell Tire. A vast network of moving esoteric architecture shaped by the orc and ogre sorcerers.
A salt water lake located in Hae'Xi. It is connected with a similar body of water in the Undercoast, and is the only body of water aside from Jor's Sea to pass the Gravity Flip.
the great inland lake at the center of Reeds. The largest source of sweet water on the Coast.
humans who own land.
an insult, meaning someone who's delusional and roleplays themselves into a position of power or importance. Based on LARP, meaning Life Acting Role Playing.
the Magistrate handling judiciary rulings, stockades, archiving, culture, the Urthguard, as well as anything esoteric. Currently held by Swentil Alemikan in the Palace District.
not a real language, but rather a way of communicating legal concepts, often in documents and contracts.
a second level Abjuration spell. It removes one affliction upon a target.
a term for the mythological chaotic sea serpent roaming Jor's Ocean. The name originates from human cults that worshipped it during the Divine Era.
honor lost by following your sworn edict, or honor lost by breaking your word.
an undead spellcaster of immense power. Created and sustained by consuming the souls of mortals, they are capable of eternal life and terrifying esoteric might.
a mortuary. A holding space for dead bodies.
Lichhouse Blues is  major quest in Esoteric Ebb.
The Light Crossbow is a Weapon belonging to Ôst. It can be obtained by convincing the Kobold to give it to you, or by pickpocketing him.
a serpent-esque draconic monstrosity native to Askan. You have never met one, but they are supposed to be fearsome.
a popular acidic berry, native to inland-Askanii. It is a staple in the Norvikian diet.
small port city in eastern Vourgeni. Known for its terrible wine production and infestation of talking (screaming) cockroaches.
Literature is a type of Equipable Item in Estoric Ebb.
a lionfolk kingdom that once stretched far inland in Askan. It fell before the end of the Divine Era.
a small swamp on the outskirts of Urthport. Known to hold the seat of the former Lithian Kingdom.
a 2nd level divination spell. Allows the caster to know the location and movement of a familiar object within one thousand feet.
a children's magazine from two decades ago. It was banned after the production company had a scandal involving bribes from the anti Bone Purge community.
the last anointed paladin of Urth. One of the original faces of the Party of Norvik.
the commander of city forces and top cleric beneath Urth. The title is appointed by Urth.
Lord Âkesam is the current Lord Patriarch of Norvik, the commander of city forces and highest-ranking cleric of Urth. He is the leader of the Party Of Norvik.

just like it sounds, this is the place in Housing where, almost like a ravine, the ruins fade away and a scar in the earth open up. The five or so blocks found within are pretty much without sun or moonlight, due to the steep surrounding slopes and tall, leaning (unregulated) buildings.
a term for the parts of the Palace District that are located in the City Below. Most of it has been civilized over the last few decades, but expansion has slowed since Urth's passing.
elephant folk. Known for the massive size, and ability to keep growing their entire lives.
big name reporter for the Post. She writes articles about all kinds of esoteric bullshit, and is able to turn anything into a compelling story.
someone your Patriarch quotes a lot. His ideas modernized theocracies in Reeds, reviving them towards the end of the Arcane Era.
magically gender-affirmed individuals.
stands for Mass Hag Recruitment. Was an initiative taken by several city-states during the Coast War to forcibly recruit and train young girls into hagdom.
The Mace of Terror is a Weapon found in the Crypt, behind the Skeleton.
the Great White Dragon of Old Diminor. He is famous for his paranoia causing the first outbreak of the Draconic Civil War.
a periodical publication generally containing short stories, adverts, investigative journalism, and/or erotica.
a common arcane cantrip. Can be used to grab things from afar.
one of the most famous universities on the Coast. Situated in New Thazen.
five rulers of the five districts. They are the Coinlord, Homelord, Shieldlord, Foodlord, and Lawlord.
the great spell of a wizard. A term used to describe Arcanists who have achieved ultimate wizardry.
#REDIRECT Arcanists
a hag-centered organization in Old Diminor focused on creating artificial population booms among the Arcanists. While successful, it led to many feminist insurgencies during their reign.
#redirect Esoteric Ebb Wiki
dwarven word, meaning approximately something akin to 'power' or 'control'.
an infamous spell that causes all testicles around to twist, turn, and... clench.

the point at which a certain valuable material used in component costs loses its perceived value to such a degree that it is no longer usable in spells. While this breaking point has been estimated in the past, the few magocrats powerful enough to research this did not publicize their findings.
a very large coven, run like a corporation. Usually working alongside their chosen city states.
like a storm, but mega. Don't happen too often.
an elemental spirit, formed by primal energies. They are known to be selfish and violent, yet useful when captured by controlling magic, or given the right incentive.
aquatic fish-people. They are found mostly around the islands and underwater nations of the Middle Edge.
half-fish folk. Not always female, but still called mermaid by humans, to the chagrin of their men.
a male merfolk. Not to be confused with mermaid.
a way of measuring distances. Invented by an Arcanist named Upplind Hagginshags.

a point deep within Jor's Ocean, around the gravity flip, where aquatic folk have built cities within stable water bubbles. A handful of pipe-mountains pierce these bubbles, allowing dry connections with the Undersea and Great Ocean islands.
Léxandro's dream of an empire to last throughout the Eras. He knew well how the Arcanists, despite their greatness, could not last past their prime.
horrifying shapeshifting monster. Capable of copying the looks of any object it discovers, although with some size limitations, depending on the type.
a common term for bovinefolk. A rare folk upon the Coast, mostly found in Hae'xi and inland Vourgeni.
a unique and mass-produced polymorphic potion that swaps the drinker upon Jor's masculinity/femininity spectrum.
Miska Sageleaf (often referred to as Lady Sageleaf) is the leader of the Halfway Bridge Tribe and administrator of Lower Tolstad.
Miss Nearly is a goblin bard.
Missing Paladin is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
Modissa is a human and a member of the Azgalian Worker's Party in Tolstad.
Money Talks is one of two possible Feats obtained from the quest Circle Square Conflict.
short for Monstrosity. Defined as a non-sentient creature born out of magical means, often by combining various natural beasts, or from curses, or Esoteric Events.
the light of the night. The great source of power that moves alone across the sky at night.
Moongore is a bugbear enforcer of Miska Sageleaf. Nessan instructs The Cleric to find her to get the Goodapple spell.
the name of a High-Corporation in Thazen, Reeds. One of the largest industrial conglomerates across the Coast.
a fighting technique pioneered by halflings during the Coast War - gripping the blade and striking with the pommel or crossguard. It is most useful against enemies in magical plate armor.
a type of sand elemental. Known for capturing unsuspecting victims and slowly draining their blood, before using their dying screams to attract more victims.

in 25 PE (two years ago) a reported fight with a local Arcanist occurred in the northernmost Norvikian settlement of Urthport. Ever since the city has been overrun with mules, which the locals are unable to get rid of.
former leader and founder of the Azgal Youth. Currently the only female foreman among the Norvik Azgal Council.
Norvik Azgal Council. The top brass behind those silly workers' union and the whole Azgalist movement in Norvik.
Non-disclosure agreement. A document that forces you to shut up about something.
NikMullach's Below Tribe. One of three goblin tribes in Norvik, helmed by the NikMullach family.
#REDIRECT Party Of Norvik
National Azgalism. The future of Norvik.
A magic item that was mass-produced during the Coast War. They were necklaces with a number of beads on them.
#REDIRECT Nessan
Nes'Wikkala (also known as Nessan) is a Pimeä Elves druid that came to Tolstad from the Undercoast. She resides in the Goblin Garden
a small village on the road through Tol's Pass.
the second biggest newspaper in the city. Freestrider aligned.
Nicholas Darrow is the Coinlord of Norvik and the magistrate of Tolstad. As a Coinlord, he handles minting, trade, financing of public works, and tithes.
a term for a goblin spellcaster. It probably started out with negative connotations.
a legendary arcanist who built the Undying Legions during the Coast War. Often called the father of modern necromancy and its many practical applications.
one of five districts in Norvik. Known as the industrial district for its many factories built along the river.
The central city of Askanii.
sometimes called the NAC, this is the group of foremen, or forefolk, guiding the Azgal movement in Norvik. They lead workers' union movements, Azgal rallies, and the entire Azgalist Workers' Party.
the official currency of Norvik. Suffered heavy inflation during the years after Urth's Passing.
the biggest newspaper in the city. Founded over fifty years ago.
Norvikian Resistance is a major quest in Esoteric Ebb.
an infamous aberration-type creature. It is said to be created when a wizard falls too deep into the rabbit hole of forbidden knowledge.
the ideology of the common gods. When deities walked among us, they built many a great empire, including Norvik.
nobody calls it that.
short for One-Sided Offspring Effect.
a legal agreement either spoken aloud or written down. Some oaths, especially those sworn on the names of deities, have real esoteric power over one's body and mind.
the Hero of Waterlane! At least, that's what the papers call him.
the only part of the Old Wall still standing in full. It stands at an impressive height of 90 feet, and casts shadow upon the streets below.
today it's called Reeds. They changed the name because, well, the entire Band was pretty fucked up after the Coast War.
Olzis is a Gynosphinx who lives in the Drunk Sphinx tavern. She is the tavern's namesake.
singular object.
an Esoteric Pocket category where one rule of reality, magic, or otherwise is changed. Sometimes in subtle ways, such as slightly lowering gravity or speeding up time.
The Orange Ledger is a work of Literature located in
big brutes of devilish origin. They're rare in Norvik.
The Order of Arcane Clerics of Norvik (abbr. OACN, also known as The Towers) are a section of the Urthguard which specializes in spellcasting.

when you picture a knight, a holy warriors acting as the moral cornerstone of any number of civilizations in Old Diminor, this is where it all began. The First Knights sadly disbanded during the early Coast War, but their ideals still live on to this day.
the great troll warlord, and one of the last holdouts against Urth. His name still haunts Tol's Pass, where the few trolls remaining in Askan plot their revenge.
#REDIRECT Ôst

the term used to describe the actual gods, those who invaded The Coast during the Gate War. They hold no dominion on Jor's Realm since the gates closed, but the most common consensus among scholars is: all walking gods are simply avatars of these real, outer ones.
a term for Illym diplomats in foreign lands. It's their way of not having to officially establish embassies.
Oyugüi is a Kargameshian Halfling Vendor found outside the Temple of Urth.
Pest Control Guild.
PPG Documents are a piece of Literature. They can be taken from Alfoz by succeeding on an Intelligence check, or pickpocketed from him at DC 9.
a focused newspaper column featuring the opinions of a writer on an topic of relevance to a targeted audience.
Party Of Norvik (also known as The Nationalists) are a Norvikian political party and the current ruling party of the city.
sounds like a hoot.
the inheritors of Urth's will. A theocratic organization founded to uphold the old ways.
a top clerical rank in Norvik. Different from Lord Patriarch, which is the traditional administrative leader of the city.
the third Lord Patriarch of Norvik. Died only four years into his service, but was instrumental in establishing a foothold upriver.
the second Lord Patriarch of Norvik. Appointed by Urth after the death of the First Patriarch.
the head of the State of Norvik, and leader of the Party of Norvik. He is an old man, and is not involved with campaigning.
Good shit.
elvish for 'goblin'.
the currency of the Vyz-Kha Kingdoms, as well as several dwarven fiefdoms on the Undercoast. It is a commonly accepted coinage in Norvik.
one of the oldest guilds in Norvik. They are supposed to keep monsters at bay, and clean up any unwanted critters across the city.
The Petrified Longbow is a Weapon found in the Roots. It is taken from the petrified body of a long-dead adventurer.
a 4th level Illusion spell. Causes untold horrors to appear before the target, tapping into their deepest fears.
the substance found naturally in the Beyond. Due to the nature of its existence, it is difficult to investigate.
Phoenix Folio is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
on exactly twenty eight days before Askanii autumn, a phoenix passes over Norvik, going inland. For an hour, the intense light turns night to day.

The Pickaxe of Secrets is a Weapon obtained by talking to Akzel Mâddson after completing the quest Lost Mines of Toul'Amon. It is an ordinary pickaxe which has been enchanted with the spell Detect Secret.
The Piece of Paper is a piece of Literature found on a corpse in the City Below the City.

an esoteric relic left after the end of the Gate Era. There are eight of these pillars around the Coast (or four, if you believe the theories of a wrapping circular Coast!) and they are indestructible.
the elven enclaves who live on the Undercoast. Also known as 'dark elves', although that's a bit on the nose.
Pínja'Athena is an enigmatic elf found behind a house in Tolstad North, silently looking at River Torna. Pinja speaks in cryptic, wistful phrases, and as such it is hard to characterize her.
#REDIRECT Pinja

Pixie Dust Slush is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Dexterity by +1 for a week. 4 Pixie Dust Slushes can be found around Tolstad: one can be stolen from a cupboard within the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building, one can be pickpocketed at DC 14 from Miska Sageleaf, one can be found in...
a popular magic item. Mass-produced during the late Arcane Era, but is in dwindling supply these days.
Esoteric Ebb features several political parties that struggle for influence in the newly democratic city of Norvik.
the illegal act of changing one's identity in order to circumvent governmental oversight, such as avoiding taxes, jail time, or other mandatory regulation.

the ideology of scientific truth, that all things, magical or otherwise, can be explained and rationalized. This style of thought was born during the early days of the Arcane Era, to counteract the barbarism of Numenism.
nickname for someone who reads the Norvikian Post.
The current era, which has lasted twenty-seven years. It succeeds the Arcane Era.
any magical substance ingested for temporary effects.
Potions of Healing are healing Consumables found in various places around Tolstad and the City Below. One Potion of Healing can be found in a crate of potions in The Sewers.
a very rare and powerful potion. It extends the drinkers natural lifespan.
one of Jor's laws of magic. In short, certain chemical compounds react strongly to Jor's strings, often causing volatile reactions.
an early Coast War class of soldiers. Obsolete with the invention of the spell 'Heat Powder'.
a series of powerful spells that focus entirely on enforcing a single reality-shift upon a target. Originally created by dragons, whose magic was verbal only.
one of the lost Power Words. There are theories on its existence among scholars, mostly focusing on the dragon's search to surpass Jor.
the Power Word everyone has heard about. Famously used by the lich Dongelshire, ending the short but well-known reign of Brian Prax.
a very convenient, but difficult to master, transmutation cantrip. It can do pretty much anything, from heating tea or cleaning clothes, to creating minor sensory effects or temporary objects.
one of the three pillars of magic. It is represented in the natural, wild, savage, and elemental forces of Jor.
Said to be the earliest and most feral type of magic.
the first son of Esuth, one among a dozen of great red dragons who all fell before their father did. This name, despite his importance during his lifetime, is but a footnote in draconic lore.

an underground city found at the deeper level of Lake Primus, right upon the Gravity Flip. Very little is known about it, except the few expeditions into its tunnels have yielded great treasures - and many lost parties.
a rule of alignment. If an action is taken for the benefit of a target, then if said target is closer emotionally to you, it will result in a less good action.
a controversial and modern form of mind healing. Based on various methods proposed by the famous Scholar Cividix.
Quests offer XP, rewards, and opportunities to explore and affect the world. Esoteric Ebb's main quest is The Tea Shop that Blew Up, but there are also dozens of side quests (some tracked, some not).
Revive Fund.
a term for Radical Azgalists. Some scary types in those crowds.
a fifth level necromancy spell. It raises a creature from the dead, if the time of passing is within ten days.
Evocation cantrip. Commonly used for offensive ranged strikes as well as other more utilitarian functions.
Razz is a goblin mercenary. He is a goblin supremacist, and will not talk to you unless you have Snell as a companion.
#REDIRECT The Coast
Reeds is one of the eight bands along The Coast. It is viewed as the homeland of Humans.
a seventh level transmutation spell. One of the few higher tier magics still commonly in use by clerics and druids across the Coast.
The Resistance Note is a piece of Literature. It can be found on the roof of the Rollermill.
a series of spells which cure various ailments. The most common are Lesser Restoration and Greater Restoration.
the act of returning a dead person back to life. There are many spells that do this, from a weak Revivify, to the legendary True Resurrection.
a type of life insurance. Monthly payments are made into an account that can be used to pay for the resurrection of the fallen holder, given they're under the age of 75 (human scale).
Revivify is a necromantic spell. It can be found in Visken's Lair, behind an illusory wall that can be detected by Detect Secrets.
Revolutionary Freestriders are a Norvikian political party and a wider ideology promoting electoral democracy and laissez-faire capitalism.
slang for resurrection. Duh.
dwarven for 'kingdom'.
The Ring of Protection is a Trinket that can be found in a birds nest in a tree in Southern Tolstad.
good food and fresh crayfish by River Torna is served by various gatherings. The kids are supposed to run or bike along the river as the ships race each other.
River Take Ye is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
A river in Askanii. It is the second longest known river on the Coast, and splits Askanii in half.
a company focused on fishing and faring on the river.
Rixbiter is the Tiefling proprietor of the Drunk Sphinx, a tavern in Tolstad.

before the eternal darkness and intense cold of Askanii winter begins, a group of women are selected in Norvik to perform a series of chants on the banks of River Torna. It is said that in the past, these women would be allowed to walk upon the water and cross the river through their love of Urth.
a gigantic bird, a beast born through esoteric means. There are a handful of them found throughout the skies of Askanii.
Stenbusken is the leader of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.
The Roper is a hostile monster located in the South Caverns. It is initially disguised as a stalagmite, but will awaken and attack the Cleric if they wander close to it.
Rubrick Prax of House Prax is according to Intellgence the last living member of the magocratic family. He is the leader of The Arcane Circle, an Arcanist political group.
the second to last port city up Torna, and final stop on the journey to Boldpoint, in Tol's Pass.
means voting card in fancy Askanii dwarven.
the legendary dwarven family who ruled before and after the AFF. Today, their leader Ketel Røykfallson holds the Forgethrone and has managed to somehow keep the federated Union of Halls intacts.
the largest dwarven empire on the Undercoast. It has stood for a thousand years, in various forms, and even survived the short-lived Azgalist revolution.
stands for service contract.
Safe & Secure is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
The Sallet is a Helmet found in the Askanii Reeds Trading Company Warehouse, in a crate of Mosquito Sand.
the central sweetwater lake of Tol's Pass.
one of the most famous port cities. Nested on an island right off the Hae'Xi mainland.
a mythical warrior caste within the mysterious Japanese culture. Little is known about them, but that hasn't stopped anyone from speculating about their origins in magazine serials.
the native name for the 'Wind-Crack Mountains'.
the third biggest free city in Reeds. Known for their divine roots, naval expertise, and vineyards.
a type of bard from Vourgeni, known for shouting spells through the valleys and across the floating landscapes.
A giant seagull can be found in the northernmost bridge of Tolstad.
aliases: The Tea Shop that Blew Up

description: each band has its own rules for the changing seasons. On the specific days, the weather is supposed to change drastically and immediately.
a term for travel towards the Coast, and towards Jor's Point.
The Secret Tunnel is a location in Tolstad North. It is a small tunnel that connects the Temple of Urth to the Tea Shop.
the worst type of cleric gig. Seriously.
Sending is a third level Evocation spell. A weakened version of it can be cast using the Trinket Sending Pebble.
a house full of clerics casting the Sending spell all day. An important tool for long-distance communication.
The Sending Pebble is a Trinket found in the dark room on the ground floor of the Rollermill. It can be used to send short messages to various other characters.
a written declaration of an oath. Often used when those involved don't have a trusted deity or higher power to swear on.

on the seventh Bloodnight you're supposed to stay quiet, and not cause any trouble, as the streets of Norvik are filled with the ghosts of the fallen. Of course, most children love to interact with the harmless ghosts, and usually spend all evening playing games with the spirits.
fun!
A walking god who lived early in the Divine Era. He was most well known for his legion of Death Knights, and for their almost century long reign over Old Diminor.
a first level abjuration spell. Can be cast as a reaction to raise an arcane barrier around the caster, protecting against the fiercest of blows.
the Magistrate handling industry, armories, city defenses, mining and refining, as well as resource allocation. Currently held by Eirik Jarn in the North Wall District.

a deep cavernous part of the City Below, found underneath the eastern districts of Norvik. It stretches far down all the way to the Gravity Flip, and is the only discovered part of the City Below to do so.
a city in the far-north of mid-Reeds. Built out of an ancient esoteric pocket, making the gravity flip around a central point.
the currency of Thazen, during the Arcane Era. It's still in use in some places, but these magical crystalline discs are only supported by a handful of cities in Reeds.

an ancient ruined city, hidden under the marshlands of central Reeds. It was abandoned towards the end of the Draconic Civil War, and very few have managed to ever gain entrance- and ever fewer have ever returned from delving into its trapped and cursed halls.
Simii is a dwarf from Tolstad, part of the Azgalists and seen with Alfoz during Circle Square Conflict. His title says "son of Strokeback".
The Simple Hammer is a Weapon. It can be found on the floor outside of Visken's Lair after resolving Circle Square Conflict.
Sir Triban is a black cat located in South Tolstad near Our Lady's Apartments.

the first noble house in to be raised by Urth after the founding of Norvik. Famously formed during a party where Johilda, the Bard with the Silver Voice, performed for Urth and he enjoyed it so much that he immediately named her nobility and gave her a piece of the city to watch over.
someone who partakes in the dangerous act of travel-onboard-wooden-board. A dumb (and kind of rad) fad.
a magazine written by a group of rogue teenage girls. Mostly contains fan fiction of famous characters from other popular magazines, only here they make them kiss and hold hands and stuff.
dwarvish for 'hurry up, you bastards'.
sausage made from gamey meat scraps. A classic among hunters across the Coast.
slightly outdated slang for oral sex.
The Sloshy Stress Cube is a Trinket.

term: Smash it with an adamantine hammer
description: a saying that means, more or less, 'doing something useless'. Since adamantine is so light, that any blunt weapon forged with it will literally just bounce off its target.

in 26 PE (last year) an anti-magic snail snuck into the Palace vault's magical barriers and accidentally took Urth's Helm, the most holy artifact in all of Norvik. It was a month long scandal covered by the press in detail as adventurers delved deep underground in search for the snail.
Snell is Cleric's goblin companion investigating the tea shop explosion on behalf of the HWBT. He is a possible party member, and the one most likely to be encountered first.
Snell's Notepad is an equipable Key item held by Snell. It can be pickpocketed at DC 7.
Snell's Pad is a location in the Holes, a series of stacked underground apartments near Waterlane. It is where Snell lives.
Snell's Notepad is an equipable Key item held by Snell. It can be pickpocketed at DC 7.
Snell's Pad is a location in the Holes, a series of stacked underground apartments near Waterlane. It is where Snell lives.
#REDIRECT Snell's Notepad
a currency made out of magical pebbles, used in various cities across Hae'Xi, Kargamesh, as well as across the Middle Edge.
Snurre Appledent is a halfling scholar in Waterlane. He is a Scholar of the Fordnip University, specializing in the field of AIS(Alignment Intent Studies).
Snurre's Office is a location in Waterlane. It is an office owned by Snurre Appledent, who can be found within it.
Snurre's Office is a location in Waterlane. It is an office owned by Snurre Appledent, who can be found within it.
the softer form of Azgalism. While Azgal envisioned a stateless, classless society, with complete equity for all, socialism is a vision of communal ownership.
the currency of the Golden Horde. A faintly self-glowing square coinage, a mixture of Amber and Gold.
the need for a physical component of a spell. It could be a hand sign, a full body movement, or something small like a wink, depending on the spell.
The South Caverns are a sub-section of the City Below the City. They are inhabited by a Roper, and are located in the domain of the HWBT.
Speak With Dead is a necromantic spell. It can be taught to you by Haelon Bondavol.
a first level divination spell. Grants you the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts.
a third level necromantic spell. Allows you to animate a spirit into a corpse and have it answer five questions, as long as that corpse retains a suitable vessel.
The Spectral Halberd is a Weapon obtained after inserting the Jacinth into the Whitemane Shrine in the South Caverns.
2nd level transmutation spell. Allows the target to move across vertical surfaces with ease and speed, by turning their limbs sticky and powerful.
a second level transmutation spell. It allows the caster to engrave a spoken message onto a surface.
dwarven term, translating into something like, 'poor little one', or 'woe be unto thee'.
one of the main rows of Housing. Full of theatres, with shows playing almost every evening of the week.
the occurrence of distant celestial bodies appearing upon the sky. If antesepalian witnesses are to be believed, many old-worlds had skies filled with thousands of these gleaming lights.
Stenbusken is a member of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.
you have heard of it.

the great prison-dungeon of Norvik. Comprised of a large section of the part of City Below that lays beneath the Palace, refitted to be used for modern rehabilitation and confinement of all types of convicted criminals.

Strange Mushroom Juice is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Wisdom by +1 for a week. Two bottles of Strange Mushroom Juice can be found across Tolstad: one can be taken from a nature spirit beneath the Imposing Tree, provided the player has Druidcraft, and one can be bought from the Potion...
the most basic type of Urthguard member, trained to patrol the streets, deal with petty crimes, and generally act as THE LAW in every day life on the streets of Norvik. They are lead by Top Watchers.
Strength (abbr. STR) is a character statistic, and a character in The Cleric's mind.
The Stress Cube is a Trinket obtained after passing a dexterity check in upper level of the Northwest Tolstad Rollermill.
Jor's Strings.
a famous comic book series, based on a merc group in Reeds. Knows for their outlandish melodrama and 'love scenes'.

an activist group formed by dwarven women in the early days of the Suffrage movement. When human women began joining the group, they eventually won over the Palace, and Universal Suffrage was ordered.
