the light of the day. The great source of power that moves alone across the sky over the day.
a brachen-chopping community, far inland in Tol's Pass. A dangerous place, known to attract the most powerful of lumberjacks.
the largest port city in Divinii Torum. Trades a ton with Norvik, at least in the last few years.

one of the original Urthfolk houses to found Norvik with Urth. Their family split off into several branches and their nobles can be found both as guild leaders, upriver manor-heads, and as one of the Magistrates.
magazine containing short stories about adventurers around the Coast.
a Kargameshian novel about the birth of the Borderlands Country. It's one of those books you learn to appreciate as you get older.
motorized armored vehicle employed during the late Coast War. They are famous for their anti-magic shields, being often used to push through Abjuration lines.
a small mining village connected to Urthport. You read an article once about them dealing with an esoteric pocket in their mines.
They Tavern Key is an equipable Key Item given by Olzis in the Drunk Sphinx during dialogue.
Temple of Urth is a location in Tolstad North. It is a small religious site dedicated to the god Urth, now housing the upcoming elections and an entrance to The City Below.
the currency of the Freestrider Revolution. While Free Cities have multiple currencies, the magical paper money of Reeds is quickly growing into the most valuable across the Coast.
Thalassos is a merman entrepreneur based in Tolstad. He is not present in the demo, only mentioned by Arn as the owner of his kiosk.
Thaumaturgy is a cantrip. It can be taken off of The Duck in the Crypt at Night after defeating the Skeleton.
Thazen was the greatest of the floating cities. During the Coast War, it was used as a center of operations by the Haagin-Dim Coalition.
#REDIRECT Kraaid
a small political party in Norvik. In the latest poll, they had a half percent of the votes.
a dwarven holiday that's celebrated by all in Norvik. Families are supposed to invite their neighbours and celebrate and grieve the expulsion of Azgal from his underground kingdom.
The Azgal Chant is a piece of Literature. It is a local Azgalian newspaper.
the dungeons and caverns beneath Norvik yet to be conquered by the Urhfolk. You will surely see that all of its glory and treasure will return to the chosen people.
The Cleric is Esoteric Ebb's player character. His full name is Ragn Hemlin, though most of the narration in the game refers to him as The Cleric.
a term for the Closing of the Gates, the event which ended the Gate Era. Occurred exactly 2027 years ago.
The Esoteric Coast (also known as Realm of Jor, Jor's Realm, or JOR) is the name of the dimension created by the wizard Jor. It is the world in which Esoteric Ebb takes place.
The term for the human founding of the city-state of Norvik, in 468 AE. Category:History
The Duck is a solemn armored warrior found in the Temple of Urth.
the anonymous and faceless Lord Patriarch who Urth appointed upon the reconquest of Norvik. He served the city for fourteen years, overseeing its founding.
a popular tavern on the border between Housing and The Palace. It's built around an old temple with a stable esoteric pocket inside- one causing the upper half of the chamber to be gravity flipped.
the catchphrase of the church of Lady Fair and Those Not.
a niche paper focused on its support of the Agricultural Party. The only major Norvikian paper printed outside of the city.
one of three unofficial districts in Norvik. Basically the halfling district.
a common slang for the central spire of apartments in Tolstad. The lower ones are less fancy.
one of five districts in Norvik. The poorer area on the west side of the river.

starting at approximately a thousand or so miles from the Coast (depending on various factors) this land is inhospitable, unexplorable, and quite frankly cursed. Each band has its own flavor of this waste.
an Exile village in Tol's Pass and main connection between the Vyz-Kha and Urthport.
The Lost 6th Edition is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
a common way to referer to Illym the Mender, the goddess who established the Illym Empire. She was kind of a big deal, before she died last century.

The Mind Expansion is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Intelligence by +1 for a week. Two bottles of The Mind Expansion can be found across Tolstad: one can be pickpocketed at DC 15 from Visken, and one can be bought from the Potion Seller for 290 Crowns.
#REDIRECT Party Of Norvik
an unofficial nickname for the current rulers of Norvik. Named after the Palace District, in which most central authority resides.
one of five districts in Norvik. Home of aristocrats, bureaucrats, and the remnants of Urth's Court.
a historical event in Norvik. It began with Urth's Landing in 466 AE, and is said to have been finished by 468 AE, when Urth finished eradicating the trolls who roamed in the ruins of Toulin'catl.
The Sewers are a sublocation of the City Below the City.
one of three unofficial districts in Norvik. Basically the farmers district.

term: The Tea Shop that Blew Up
description: The Tea Shop that Blew Up is the first and main quest of the game.
the home base of the Order of Arcane Clerics of Norvik. Also known as the OACN.
The Undercommon is an equippable piece of Literature. It can be found in the Tea Shop on a burnt bookshelf.
a place for community and socialization that is not the home or work.
a collection of laws surrounding the lawful arrest of citizens.
Thomas Darrow was a nobleman of House Darrow and the uncle of Nicholas Darrow. He was a vampire.
an offensive Transmutation cantrip. Often used by druids as a medium-range spell attack.
Three Hundred Pieces Ye is minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
the name of the birdfolk tribes which ruled far inland in Divinii Torum. They unified 57 years ago, and created a sovereignty based on Azgalian ideas.
a folk with demonic or devilish blood in their veins. Often have features such as horns, tails, red or blue skin color, and various esoteric abilities.
god of beasts, tannery, and trade among the Viking-folk of Old Diminor. She is seen as a forgotten god in the Post-War Era.
a classic line from the play 'Tysseus', about a merman who decides to be swim around the Coast. It is considered bigoted by some, as it does not portray merfolk culture in any semblance of accuracy.
Tinnitul is a Smoke Mephit in the employ of the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company. He is bound to the fireplace of the Akanii-Reeds Trading Company Building in Waterlane.
The Tiny Dictator is a Trinket found in Snurre's Office.
the only major town between the Fordnip Commonwealth and Vyz-Kha.
Tisereldanour was a "great" Green Dragon and a draconic ruler. She ruled the area of the Illym Forest.
term for the gigantic creatures who first inhabited and shaped Jor's Realm.
the tax collecting guild. Each district has its own.
Tokig the Gambler was a trickster god and an ascended goblin.
a trickster goblin who united the tribes of Askanii during the Divine Era. Most famous for cutting off his right hand to fulfill a prophecy.
Tol was the brother of god Urth. He was a mythical explorer of Askan who founded several towns along the River Torna, including Tolstad.
The Tol's Sword is a Weapon found after using the Deck of Many Things, and receiving permanent disadvantage on everything.
a holiday for lovers and young people, who meet up at various fields outside the city to dance through the night. You were never invited to one.
a Norvikian holiday. To honor Urth's brother Tol as he left to explore the wastes.

the valley containing the inland-end of River Torna, as well as the farthest stretches of Norvikian civilization. Known for its rare mineral deposits, the terrible Barnacle Goose, and almost as horrific Esoteric Storms.
The Tol's Sword is a Weapon found after using the Deck of Many Things, and receiving permanent disadvantage on everything.
Toli Tolpare is a member of the Norvikian Resistance. He can usually be found in the Rollermill or Tavern with his friends.
Tolstad is the central district of Norvik, and the setting of the game. It is an island fortress on River Torna.
basically a bunch of goblins keeping monsters from entering from the Undercoast Paths. They also do firefighting and are mostly funded by the HWBT.
a third level divination spell. It grants a target the ability to understand all languages, and for the target's speech to be understandable by any sentient listener.
high ranking Urthguard member working as a leader of a unit of Street Watchers. Many of them are low-ranking clerics, in order to leverage magic in their criminal investigations.

to hold the darkness of autumn at bay, a great bonfire in placed on top a longship and lit on fire in Norvik. It is then sailed north on River Torna, where it is re-lit in every major settlement in the coming weeks.
the evil fungal god of the depth. A great warrior and conqueror who rages across the Undercoast.
a term for a non-divine inhabitant of Divnii Torum.
The mollusca Toulin'catl were an ancient Askanii civilization of snailfolk. They disappeared during the Fade Era.
The mollusca Toulin'catl were an ancient Askanii civilization of snailfolk. They disappeared during the Fade Era.
a term borrowed from Anuran. It is translated as 'Feat of Strength'.
The Tower Key is an equipable Key Item given by Nicholas Darrow during dialogue. It can also be taken from him without talking to him.
a title afforded by the Free Cities to the executives within the High-Corporations. Essentially, one of the big players who run the show in Reeds.
a rare class of aberration found across the Coast. They are known to run various cults across the Undersea, often trading in information.
a monster native to Askan. They exist in broad categories, from small and mostly harmless green trolls to mutated volatile curse trolls.
The Troll Tooth is a Trinket left behind by Big Leader Gourgalash after he is defeated.
a house founded by Noppe, a peasant who slaughtered several trolls alongside Urth during the conquest. He was granted a high position in the city due to his bravery.
a small forest located west of Norvik. It is infamous for still having a small clan of green trolls living within its borders.

a ninth level legendary spell, capable of bringing anyone dead back to life, if you believe the legends. There are many different tales with different takes on the restrictions, but generally you cannot use it on a creature that has been dead for over two hundred years.
an old play about a kingdom run by a group of idiotic advisors. Also popularized the term 'two crowns'.
merfolk for 'great'.
the jewel of Kargamesh. Means 'endless pillars' in primordial.
the All-Wise one. The black dragon of Old Diminor, known for holding the largest dragondom at his peak.
Undersea Coast is a part of the Jor's Realm that is found underneath its surface.
the dwarves of the Undercoast. Their own term for it would be 'Glôddvaerg'.
the gravity reversed cavern, sea, and archipelago. Right underneath the Coast.
an equine-like celestial with a magical horn on their forehead. They are known to frequent various forested areas, and are often mistaken for fey.
the federation of multiple Undercoast kingdoms under the Røykfall Empire.
The Unluck Blade is a Weapon located in the Undercoast Path. It is dropped by the Assassin after they are defeated for the final time.
the underground half-mountain jutting out close to the Gravity Flip, right underneath Norvik. It is the area connecting parts of the City Below with the deeper Undercoast.
Urth was the state god of Norvik, known by his epithet as the Father Of Norvik. He is The Cleric's god after whom the Urthguard are named, as well as the human ethnicity Urthfolk.
Norvikian lingo. Spoken as an exclamation of surrender to the knowledge and power of the protective god of the city.
Urth's Passing is a religious observance related to the god Urth. It is a winter celebration where families feast together.
Urth's Passing is a religious observance related to the god Urth. It is a winter celebration where families feast together.
the people of Urth. The ancestors of those who reconquered the Askanii coast.
Urthguard in Norvik are the main law-keeping organization; the guards of the city. They are linked to the religion of the god Urth.
the northernmost town under Norvikian influence, and the last civilized stop along the river. Famous for its furs, expeditions, and of course the Muletown Incident.
a dwarven expression of disgust.
short for Vetta Dei Re, a small Askanii city state known for its griffon racing. Real gambling palace, if you trust the stories.
dwarven slang for Vyz-Kha. As if it needed to be shortened.
Valkyrie is a minor quest in Esoteric Ebb.
the world is highly complicated.
Mind of Shadows. A type of lichdom created through primal stasis, usually by powerful druids or elven wizards.
Tolstad is full of tiny holes and air shafts. These circulate air from the river winds all throughout the tunnels.
a minor but wealthy gnomish settlement east of Norvik. They breed racing griffons, whom many journey to see during Game Season.
the folk of Old Diminor, who lived scattered among the western shores. Those few who survived the creation of the Curseland followed Urth to form the city of Norvik.

Visken is Tolstad's mortician and former dentist and surgeon. He is the intended first character that the player talks to, and provides useful information about the main quest, Cleric's immediate past and items, information about the city, and starts quests associated with him.
a quality classification of edged weaponry. Many Vorpal Swords were made for officers in the Coast War, but many did not survive into the new era due to poor craftsmanship.
The Voting Card is a Literature item. It can be found in the lower layer of Visken's Lair, next to the staircase leading up.
Vourgeni is one of the eight bands along The Coast. It neighbors Hae'Xi and Reeds.

The Vourgeni River Droplets are a consumable that increases the Cleric's Strength by +1 for a week. 3 bottles of Vourgeni River Droplets can be found across Tolstad: one under a stone in Waterlane that can be detected by Find Secrets, one that can be bought from the Potion Seller for 220 Crowns, ...

a lost religious text originally passed down within the ranks of the Order of the First Knights in Old Diminor. These great warriors used the teachings in this secretive and holy scripture to ultimately influence all modern ideas of chivarly and knighthood.
The Vyz-Kha Kingdoms, or VK for short, are a collection of loose dwarven cities in northwestern Askanii.

The Vyz-Kha Ginger Drink is a consumable that increases the Cleric's Constitution by +1 for a week. 2 bottles of Vyz-Kha Ginger Drink can be found across Tolstad: one in a crate behind the Griffon near the Askanii-Reeds Trading Company Building, and one that can be bought from the Potion Seller f...
#REDIRECT Vyz-Kha

the term used to describe the divine avatars who rose and fell in and around the Divine Era. Most major religions on the Coast today are built around these figures, and their influence can be felt everywhere.
Urthguard members trained to defend the walls of the city. They typically deal with creatures from the wilds breaching into the districts, even the unofficial ones.
the long street going under the shadow of the Old City Wall. Famous for being one of the most tightly populated areas of the city.
Kullervo is an Elf and a representative of Illym.
Kullervo is an Elf and a representative of Illym.
a half-frozen, half-submerged spiral maze in the City Below. Found underneath the mouth of the River Torna and is said to suck in a portion of the river's water, sending it to the Undersea.

the largest trade port in Norvik, located in a massive tunnel piercing the center of Tolstad. It is a so-called 'marvel of dwarven engineering', and it's the reason so many people commute to Tolstad over the many bridges.
magazine containing information about living spaces, as well as a lot of ads from landlords.
A tribe of feline-folk who ruled portions of Askanii during the Divine Era.
that's not in any history book.
